title: For a World at War
scripture_text: He shall judge between many peoples, and shall arbitrate between strong nations far away; they shall beat their swords into plowshares.
reflection: The world is broken in ways that politics cannot fix. But the Lord is the Lord of the nations, and He has not abdicated.
body:
You saw the news. The images will not leave you. Children. Families. Cities in the wrong hands. The cruelty of it sits in your chest like a stone.

The Lord sees the same images. He is not indifferent. The cross is the proof. God Himself entered the worst of what we do to each other, and He did not do so from a throne. He did so from a victim's place.

Pray. Pray for the leaders, even the ones you cannot stand. Pray for the soldiers on all sides. Pray for the civilians, especially the children, especially the parents, especially the old.

Your prayer is not small. Your prayer joins the prayer of Jesus, who is at this moment interceding for the same people you are interceding for.
prayer: Lord of the nations, have mercy. On the children. On the soldiers. On the leaders. On us all. Bring Your peace. Amen.
